back_translation: In the seventh year, Jehoiada strengthened himself and made a covenant with the commanders of hundreds: Azariah son of Jeroham, Ishmael son of Jehohanan, Azariah son of Obed, Maaseiah son of Adaiah, and Elishaphat son of Zichri.
They went throughout Judah and gathered the Levites from all the cities of Judah, and the heads of the families of Israel, and they came to Jerusalem.
The whole assembly made a covenant with the king in the house of God. Jehoiada said to them, “Behold, the king’s son! He must reign, as the LORD promised concerning the descendants of David.”
This is what you all must do: a third of you who are priests and Levites entering duty on the Sabbath shall be gatekeepers.
Another third at the royal palace, and another third at the Foundation Gate; and all the people in the courts of the house of the LORD.
Let no one enter the house of the LORD except the priests and the Levites who perform duty; they may enter because they are holy. But all the people shall keep the charge that the LORD has appointed.
The Levites shall surround the king, each holding a weapon in his hand. Whoever enters the temple must be put to death. Stay with the king when he enters and goes out.

The Levites and all Judah did everything Jehoiada the priest commanded. Each took his men, both those entering duty on the Sabbath and those going off duty, because Jehoiada the priest did not dismiss any of the shifts.
Jehoiada the priest gave to the commanders of hundreds the spears and the large and small shields of King David that were in the house of God.
Jehoiada stationed all the people, each holding a weapon in his hand, from the south side to the north side of the temple, around the altar and the temple, surrounding the king.
Then they brought out the king’s son, put the crown on him, gave him the book of the Testimony, and made him king. Jehoiada and his sons anointed him, and shouted, “Long live the king!”
When Athaliah heard the sound of the people running and shouting blessings to the king, she came out to the people at the house of the LORD.

She looked and saw the king standing by his pillar at the entrance, with the commanders and trumpeters beside the king. All the people of the land rejoiced and blew trumpets, and the singers with their instruments led the praise. Then Athaliah tore her clothes and cried, “Treason! Treason!”

Jehoiada the priest ordered the commanders of hundreds in charge of the army, “Bring her out between the ranks of soldiers; whoever follows her, kill him with the sword,” for the priest said, “Do not kill her in the house of the LORD.”
So they made way for her, and she reached the entrance of the Horse Gate of the royal palace, and there they killed her.
Jehoiada made a covenant between himself, all the people, and the king, that they would be the people of the LORD.
All the people went to the temple of Baal and tore it down. They smashed the altars and idols to pieces, and killed Mattan the priest of Baal in front of the altars.

Jehoiada placed the oversight of the house of the LORD into the hands of the Levitical priests, whom David had appointed to oversee the house of the LORD, to offer burnt offerings to the LORD as written in the Law of Moses, with rejoicing and song as David had commanded.
He stationed gatekeepers at the gates of the house of the LORD, so that no one who was unclean for any reason could enter.

He took the commanders of hundreds, the nobles, the rulers of the people, and all the people of the land, and brought the king down from the house of the LORD. They came through the Upper Gate to the royal palace and seated the king on the royal throne.
All the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet, because they had executed Athaliah with the sword.
